howItWorks.intro: Quilt backing must be larger than the quilt top on all sides to allow for quilting shift. The backing fabric width determines whether seams are needed.
rules: Add 4 inches of overhang on each side (8 inches total to width and length)
If the backing width needed exceeds the fabric bolt width, you need seamed panels
For standard 42-44" fabric, most quilts wider than 38" need at least one seam
howItWorks.outro: Use 108-inch wide backing fabric to avoid seams on most quilts. This calculator figures out the optimal panel layout and total yardage automatically.

faq.question: How much bigger should quilt backing be than the quilt top?
faq.answer: Standard practice is 4 inches extra on each side, making the backing 8 inches wider and 8 inches longer than the quilt top. Longarm quilters often request 4-6 inches extra per side. Check with your quilter for their specific requirements.

faq.question: How do I piece quilt backing with standard width fabric?
faq.answer: Cut two or three lengths of fabric to the needed backing length, then sew them together along the selvage edges with a 1/2-inch seam. Press seams open to reduce bulk. Place seams off-center for a cleaner look.

faq.question: What is wide quilt backing fabric?
faq.answer: Wide backing fabric is typically 108 inches (3 yards) wide, specifically made for quilt backs. It eliminates the need for seams on most quilt sizes. It comes in cotton, cotton blends, and is available in many prints and solids.

faq.question: How do I calculate batting size for my quilt?
faq.answer: Batting should be the same size as your backing — quilt top dimensions plus 4 inches on each side. For a 60×80-inch quilt top, you need batting at least 68×88 inches. Buy the next standard batting size up from your needs.

faq.question: Should I pre-wash my quilt backing fabric?
faq.answer: If you plan to wash the finished quilt, pre-washing is recommended to prevent uneven shrinkage between the top and backing. Most quilting cotton shrinks 3-5%. If you want the crinkled antique look, skip pre-washing.

faq.question: How do I calculate backing for a king-size quilt?
faq.answer: A king-size quilt is typically 104×96 inches. With 4-inch overhang, you need 112×104-inch backing. With 44-inch fabric, that requires 3 widths sewn together: 3 panels × 112 inches = 9.3 yards. Or use 108-inch wide backing for just 3.1 yards with no seams.

chart.title: Standard Quilt Sizes & Backing Yardage
chart.subtitle: Yardage includes 4-inch overhang per side. Based on 44-inch fabric width.
